title: Neomammillaria lewisiana
taxonomy: Neomammillaria lewisiana is classified within the family Cactaceae, which includes other species of cacti. It belongs to the genus Mammillaria, a group of small to medium-sized cacti known for their cylindrical stems and small flowers. The species Neomammillaria lewisiana is a rare and endemic species, found primarily in Mexico. Its classification is further refined within the subgenus Cactopsis, which is a subset of the Mammillaria genus. This classification system helps to organize and understand the relationships between different species of Neomammillaria lewisiana and other related plants.

intro: Neomammillaria lewisiana, also known as the Lewis's Pincushion or Red Pincushion Cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, native to Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a useful remedy for wound healing, skin conditions, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria lewisiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and minor wounds.
